_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MCh/DNB or Equivalent (in Urology, Pediatric Surgery)
clinical_indications: Severe Benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), false urethral passages, morbid obesity, urethral strictures, bladder neck contracture
Benign hypertrophy and carcinoma of the prostate
Reduces the pre and postoperative morbidity and mortality of several diseases

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management, advise for the procedure.
USG/CT confirming the diagnosis and need for the procedure

claim.label: Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes detailing how urine was drained.

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management, advise for the procedure submitted?
Was the USG/CT diagnosis recommending for the Suprapubic drainage report submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical notes and/or USG/CT diagnosis report not indicative of the procedure.
Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management, and advice not submitted or incomplete.
USG/CT diagnosis recommending suprapubic drainage not submitted.
Detailed Indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details not submitted or incomplete.
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes detailing how urine was drained not available or incomplete.
Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge not available or incomplete.

clinical_key_pointers: Suprapubic Drainage or Percutaneous suprapubic catheterization (SPC) is a procedure for urinary drainage.
To manage bladder dysfunction and urinary retention, difficulty in urethral catheterization, a suprapubic cystostomy may be a valuable measure for emergency drainage of the bladder.
Urinary tract infection which accompanies urethral drainage is largely eliminated with Suprapubic trocar drainage of bladder.
